title: Using custom functions in `pid_pos`
text: minimal example, suppose want tag main characters Friends proper nouns. define sentence-level tagging function: use pid_pos(), wrap custom_tagger(): can now supply pid_pos():

title: Using custom functions in `pid_pos`
text: default filter_to_proper_noun() assumes: Proper nouns labeled "PROPN" POS column named "upos" tagger uses different conventions, must supply custom filter. example, suppose: Proper nouns labeled "NNP" POS column named "POS" define: compatible tagger: Now use :
code: custom_filter <- function(tag_frm) {   dplyr::filter(tag_frm, POS == "NNP") } friends_tagger2 <- custom_tagger(function(sentence) {   if (is.na(sentence)) {     return(tibble::tibble(Token = character(), POS = character()))   }    clean_text <- gsub("[[:punct:]]", "", sentence)   tokens <- strsplit(clean_text, "\\s+")[[1]]   tokens <- tokens[tokens != ""]    tibble::tibble(     Token = tokens,     POS = ifelse(tokens %in% friends, "NNP", "XXX")   ) }) pid_pos(   example.data,   tagger = friends_tagger2,   filter = custom_filter ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 7 #>    Token    POS   ID                Sentence Document Repeats `Affected Columns` #>  * <chr>    <chr> <glue>            <chr>    <chr>      <int> <chr>              #>  1 Phoebe   NNP   Col:speaker Row:2 "Phoebe… "Phoebe…       3 `speaker`          #>  2 Ross     NNP   Col:text Row:2    "Oh, Ro… "Oh, Ro…       1 `text`             #>  3 Monica   NNP   Col:speaker Row:3 "Monica… "Monica…       6 `speaker`          #>  4 Ross     NNP   Col:speaker Row:4 "Ross G… "Ross G…       3 `speaker`          #>  5 Joey     NNP   Col:speaker Row:5 "Joey T… "Joey T…       3 `speaker`          #>  6 Chandler NNP   Col:speaker Row:7 "Chandl… "Chandl…       3 `speaker`          #>  7 Ross     NNP   Col:text Row:13   "Yes! title: Using custom functions in `pid_pos`
text: tagging engine can also replaced entirely. example, Python’s nltk library provides POS tagging functionality. following Python function (included package nltk_function.py) defines simple wrapper: can bridge R using reticulate (see vignette intro using reticulate). Note: section requires working Python installation nltk module. available, code skipped.
code: from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize from nltk.tag import pos_tag  def nltk_pos_tagger(sentence):     tokens = word_tokenize(sentence)     return pos_tag(tokens) reticulate::use_virtualenv("YOUR ENVIRONMENT", required = TRUE)  if (!reticulate::py_module_available("nltk")) {   reticulate::py_install("nltk", pip = TRUE) } nltk_tagger <- custom_tagger(function(sentence) {   tagged <- nltk_pos_tagger(sentence)    tagged_frm <- tibble::as_tibble(     do.call(rbind, tagged),     .name_repair = "minimal"   )    colnames(tagged_frm) <- c("Token", "POS")    dplyr::mutate(     tagged_frm,     dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), as.character)   ) })  nltk_filter <- function(frm) {   dplyr::filter(frm, POS %in% c("NNP", "NNPS")) }  pid_pos(   example.data,   tagger = nltk_tagger,   filter = nltk_filter )

title: Reporting on every file in a folder
text: common struggle research data controllers can gain level confidence large data sets don’t contain personally identifiable data. cases, job requires brief inspection columns often contain PID name, ID obvious. However, data sets can contain broad free text fields, fields needed small number cases, may shifted - placing PID harder detect locations. data sets consist ~10,000 observations manual inspection rare PID limited chance finding problems, let alone resource cost required pass data. help overcome issues, part PID.POS package implemented API automated production proper noun reports files found within directory. intention collection data sets required transfer, can placed single location, reports generated. demonstrate function works - supplied collection data sets featuring free text package. free text data draws friends package - containing scripts three episodes season 8: One ''.csv One Rachel Tells.csv One Red Sweater.csv file consisting 6 columns: file consisting 6 columns: text - line script (dialogue action) speaker - character uttering line season - Numeric ID season episode episode - Numeric ID episode dialogue scene utterance check files intended data: generate reports call report_on_folder two key arguments: data_path - path data directory report_dir - [optional] system path proper noun reports saved evaluated report_dir folder gets populated set csv files, one per data set found data_path: files consists 6 columns: ID - reference proper noun detected Token - proper noun detected Sentence - sentence containing proper nouns Document - full document Sentence occurs . Repeats - many times Document appeared data set Affected Columns - columns Document occurred . report_on_folder function high level API pid_pos package allows various options control reporting process. optional arguments : tagger - proper noun tagger use. default “english-ewt” udpipe model, users can change either supplying character string available udpipe model (see) custom tagger function. See … details implementing custom tagger. filter_func - function takes data frame tagged tokens filters tokens interest. default, set filter proper nouns, users can implement function filter types tokens e.g. locations, dates. using udpipe taggers, upos column contains universal part speech tags can used filter wide range token types. chunk_size - number rows process time. designed help memory management processing large files, can set NULL read whole file . to_ignore - vector column names ignore searching proper nouns. designed help cases known columns contain proper nouns e.g. primary keys, name columns. default, set NULL columns processed. export_function - function takes resulting data frames exports desired location. Two export functions included package (export_as_tree reports nested folder structure raw data export_flat flatten structure capture nested nature via file naming). verbose -

title: Convert a POS tagging function to a tagger for the pid_pos package — custom_tagger
code: # Example of a POS tagging function for a single sentence proper_nouns <- c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie")  pos_function <- function(sentence) {   tokens <- unlist(strsplit(sentence, " "))    data.frame(     Token = tokens,     upos = ifelse(tokens %in% proper_nouns, "PROPN", "OTHER")   ) |>     dplyr::mutate(Sentence = sentence) }  .tagger <- custom_tagger(pos_function)  docs <- c("Alice is here", "Bob is there", "Charlie is everywhere") .tagger(docs) #>        Token  upos              Sentence ID #> 1      Alice PROPN         Alice is here  1 #> 2         is OTHER         Alice is here  1 #> 3       here OTHER         Alice is here  1 #> 4        Bob PROPN          Bob is there  2 #> 5         is OTHER          Bob is there  2 #> 6      there OTHER          Bob is there  2 #> 7    Charlie PROPN Charlie is everywhere  3 #> 8         is OTHER Charlie is everywhere  3 #> 9 everywhere OTHER Charlie is everywhere  3  doc.frm <- data.frame(Text = docs) pid_pos(doc.frm, tagger = .tagger, filter_func = filter_to_proper_nouns) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   ID             Token   Sentence            Document Repeats `Affected Columns` #> * <glue>         <chr>   <chr>               <chr>      <int> <chr>              #> 1 Col:Text Row:1 Alice   Alice is here       Alice i…       1 `Text`             #> 2 Col:Text Row:2 Bob     Bob is there        Bob is …       1 `Text`             #> 3 Col:Text Row:3 Charlie Charlie is everywh… Charlie…       1 `Text`

what: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
title: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
text: `tag_documents()` applies tagging function (defaulting `udpipe_factory()`) vector text documents, optionally splitting chunks memory efficiency. function returns tibble containing tokens, sentences, token-level metadata.

title: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
code: tag_documents(docs, doc_ids = NULL, tagger = NULL, chunk_size = 100)

title: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
text: docs Character vector. text documents tag. Must non-empty. doc_ids Character vector NULL. Optional document identifiers; NULL, default IDs generated. tagger Function. tagging function, typically created `udpipe_factory()`. chunk_size Integer. Number documents process per batch. Defaults 100.

title: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
text: tibble (`tbl_df`) columns depending `tagger`s output.

title: Tag a set of documents — tag_documents
code: # Sample text example_text <- c(   "This is a test sentence.",   "Here is another sentence." )  # Create a tagger for the English EWT model ewt_tagger <- udpipe_factory("english-ewt") ewt_result <- pid.pos:::tag_documents(example_text, tagger = ewt_tagger)  # Create a tagger for the English GUM model gum_tagger <- udpipe_factory("english-gum") gum_result <- pid.pos:::tag_documents(example_text, tagger = gum_tagger)  # Create a tagger for the English LINES model lines_tagger <- udpipe_factory("english-lines") lines_result <- pid.pos:::tag_documents(example_text, tagger = lines_tagger)

what: Create a UDPipe tagging function — udpipe_factory
title: Create a UDPipe tagging function — udpipe_factory
text: Returns function tags text documents using specified UDPipe model. returned function accepts character vector documents returns tibble tokens, sentences, token metadata.

title: Create a UDPipe tagging function — udpipe_factory
code: udpipe_factory(   model = "english-ewt",   model_dir = pid.pos_env$model_folder,   udpipe_repo = pid.pos_env$udpipe_repo )

title: Create a UDPipe tagging function — udpipe_factory
text: model Character. name UDPipe model use. Defaults `english-ewt`. model_dir Character. Directory UDPipe models stored. udpipe_repo Character. URL path UDPipe model repository.

title: Create a UDPipe tagging function — udpipe_factory
text: function takes character vector documents returns `tibble` columns: ID Document identifier Token Individual token text Sentence Sentence containing token upos universal parts speech tag token. See https://universaldependencies.org/format.html columns returned `udpipe::udpipe()` function token.
